info.name: PUBLIC Cloudbeds API V1.3
info.description:
# Welcome

Welcome to the documentation for **Cloudbeds API Version 1.3**!  
If you are looking to learn how to use the Cloudbeds API to access guest information, reservations, or similar data for your Cloudbeds customers, then you've come to the right place.

In this document you will find all the API methods we provide along with explanations for parameters and response examples.

If you you have questions about different implementation steps (e.g. how to implement authentication), please refer to our [Developers portal](https://developers.cloudbeds.com/).

**Endpoint:** [https://hotels.cloudbeds.com/api/v1.3/{method}](https://hotels.cloudbeds.com/api/v1.1/%7Bmethod%7D)

**HTTPS:** Our API requires HTTPS. We'll respond with an appropriate error if you're not using it.

**Request Format:** HTTP GET, POST, and PUT (Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded)

**Response Format:** JSON

**Response Header:** X-Request-ID is added to response headers in all calls to help accelerate support and troubleshooting.

# Recommended Articles

Before you start, make sure to read the [Integration guide](https://developers.cloudbeds.com/docs/integration-guide) and [Authentication](https://developers.cloudbeds.com/docs/authentication-1) guides in our  
[Integrations Portal](https://integrations.cloudbeds.com/hc/en-us). Read the introduction to learn about formats and limits  
and check out our [sample use cases](https://developers.cloudbeds.com/docs/use-cases-blueprints).

# Support

Have questions/comments or found a bug? Feel free to contact on integrations@cloudbeds.com. We require that the API request, response and timestamp to be submitted.

# Versioning

We avoid making backwards-incompatible changes to the API architecture. On occasion, however, we may need to deprecate  
an existing method. In these circumstances, we notify any current users of the method and provide recommendations  
for next steps as well as a sunset period to implement them.

Please refer to our [API Changelog](https://developers.cloudbeds.com/changelog) for recent updates and improvements.

# API Rate Limits

The Cloudbeds API requires the following rate limits for best experience. Exceeding these limits may result in a temporary suspension of your API credentials. If this occurs, we will notify you directly via email. Once you  
confirm you have adjusted your integration's rate intervals, we will then reactivate your credentials.

## Properties and Associations

Our limit for properties and association are the same: `5 requests per second`

## Tech Partners

Commercial technology partners have a higher rate limit: `10 requests per second`
